paper.title: Hypervelocity star candidates from Gaia DR2 and DR3 proper motions and parallaxes

extraction.summary: Extracted 72 nearest extreme HVS candidates from Table Tab_72dr3. The paper explicitly presents all 72 as HVS candidates with vtan_g > 700 km/s and parallactic distance < 4 kpc, selected from Gaia DR3. While none passed all quality checks, the paper notes that at least some still appear unbound to the Galaxy.

method_chain.summary: Selection from Gaia DR2 and DR3 astrometric catalogs with proper motions, parallaxes, and photometry.

source_refs.context: Gaia HVS selection from tangential velocities using DR2 and DR3

method_chain.summary: Astrometric quality selection: PM > 20 mas/yr, Plx > 0.1 mas, RPlx > 5 (high priority) or 3 < RPlx < 5 (low priority).

source_refs.context: Selection criteria for HVS candidates from Gaia DR3

method_chain.summary: Heliocentric distance from parallax inversion for stars with significant Gaia parallaxes.

source_refs.context: Parallax-based distance estimation

method_chain.summary: Computation of heliocentric tangential velocities vtan and Galactocentric tangential velocities vtan_g using solar motion correction with local standard of rest velocity of 235 km/s.

source_refs.context: Galactocentric tangential velocity computation

method_chain.summary: Selection of extreme HVS candidates with vtan_g > 700 km/s and parallactic distance < 4 kpc for detailed quality validation.

source_refs.context: Selection of 72 nearest extreme HVS candidates with vtan_g > 700 km/s and distance < 4 kpc

method_chain.summary: Detailed validation of 72 nearest extreme candidates using DR3 flags (Dup, IPDfmp, IPDfow, Solved), close neighbour search, and local/global comparison of ten astrometric quality parameters.

source_refs.context: Detailed validation of 72 nearest extreme HVS candidates
DR3 astrometric quality parameters and comparison methods

method_chain.summary: Adoption of radial velocity measurements from Gaia DR3 spectroscopic pipeline where available.
outputs: RV values for brighter HVS candidates

source_refs.context: DR3 radial velocity availability for HVS candidates

inclusion_assessment.summary: Candidate 001 is one of 72 nearest extreme DR3 HVS candidates with vtan_g > 700 km/s and parallactic distance < 4 kpc selected from Gaia DR3.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly labels all 72 objects as HVS candidates and studies them as possibly unbound from the Galaxy, though it notes none passed all quality checks and their tangential velocities may be overestimated.
